name: edb-debugger
title: edb-debugger
summary: edb - graphical debugger and disassembler for executables

commands.name: edb
commands.description: Graphical debugger and disassembler for executables

arguments.description: attach the process of PID <pid> to debugger
arguments.raw: --attach  attach the process of PID <pid> to debugger

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# man edb
 EDB(1)                                                                   EDB(1)
 
 NAME
    edb - graphical debugger and disassembler for executables
 
 SYNOPSIS
     edb [OPTION]... [TARGET]
 
 DESCRIPTION
    edb  (Evan's  Debugger)  is a modular and modern disassembler and debugger
    for binary ELF files based on ptrace API and the capstone disassembly  li-
    brary.
 
    --help
           Show usage and exit.
 
    --symbols <file>
           generate symbols map for file <file>
 
    --attach <pid>
           attach the process of PID <pid> to debugger
 
    --run <program> [args...]
           open <program> in debugger with optional [args...]
 
    --version
           show version string and exit.
 
    --dump-version
           show version and exit.
 
 EXAMPLE
    edb --symbols /lib/libc.so.6 > libc.so.6.map
 
         Will generate symbols for libc and save it in a text file. It's useful if you store this map files in the symbols directory configured in edb's preferences.
 
    for i in $(ls /lib); do edb --symbols $i > $(basename $i).map; done
 
          Useful to generate maps for all libs you have in /lib.
 
    edb --run /bin/ls
 
          Will open the ls program binary in debugger.
 
    edb --attach 1720
 
         Attach the process of PID 1720 to debugger.
 
 AUTHOR
    Written by Evan Teran <evan.teran@gmail.com>
 
 REPORTING BUGS
    Report    any    bugs    or    requests    for   features   via   BTS   on
    https://github.com/eteran/edb-debugger/issues
 
 COPYRIGHT
    Copyright  (C)  2008  CodeF00.  Licensed  GPLv2:   GNU   GPL   version   2
    <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/old-licenses/gpl-2.0.txt>. This is free soft-
    ware: you are free to change and redistribute it. There is NO WARRANTY, to
    the extent permitted by law.
 
                                December 2011                           EDB(1)

local_help.--help:
QStandardPaths: XDG_RUNTIME_DIR not set, defaulting to '/tmp/runtime-root'
Usage: edb [OPTIONS]

 --attach <pid>            : attach to running process
 --run <program> (args...) : execute specified <program> with <args>
 --stdin <filename>        : set the STDIN of the target process (MUST preceded --run)
 --stdout <filename>       : set the STDOUT of the target process (MUST preceded --run)
 --version                 : output version information and exit
 --dump-version            : display terse version string and exit
 --help                    : display this help and exit

BinaryInfoPlugin::BinaryInfo
 --symbols <filename>      : generate symbols for <filename> and exit

local_help.man:
EDB(1)                                                                   EDB(1)

NAME
     edb - graphical debugger and disassembler for executables

SYNOPSIS
      edb [OPTION]... [TARGET]

DESCRIPTION
     edb  (Evan's  Debugger)  is a modular and modern disassembler and debugger
     for binary ELF files based on ptrace API and the capstone disassembly  li-
     brary.

     --help
            Show usage and exit.

     --symbols <file>
            generate symbols map for file <file>

     --attach <pid>
            attach the process of PID <pid> to debugger

     --run <program> [args...]
            open <program> in debugger with optional [args...]

     --version
            show version string and exit.

     --dump-version
            show version and exit.

EXAMPLE
     edb --symbols /lib/libc.so.6 > libc.so.6.map

          Will generate symbols for libc and save it in a text file. It's useful if you store this map files in the symbols directory configured in edb's preferences.

     for i in $(ls /lib); do edb --symbols $i > $(basename $i).map; done

           Useful to generate maps for all libs you have in /lib.

     edb --run /bin/ls

           Will open the ls program binary in debugger.

     edb --attach 1720

          Attach the process of PID 1720 to debugger.

AUTHOR
     Written by Evan Teran <evan.teran@gmail.com>

REPORTING BUGS
     Report    any    bugs    or    requests    for   features   via   BTS   on
     https://github.com/eteran/edb-debugger/issues

apt_metadata.description:
cross platform x86/x86-64 debugger
edb is a graphical cross platform x86/x86-64 debugger.
It was inspired by Ollydbg, but aims to function on x86
and x86-64 as well as multiple OS's. Linux is the only
officially supported platform at the moment, but FreeBSD,
OpenBSD, OSX and Windows ports are underway with varying
degrees of functionality.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use edb because edb - graphical debugger and disassembler for executables.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `edb [OPTION]... [TARGET]` as documented upstream for edb.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `edb --symbols /lib/libc.so.6 > libc.so.6.map` as documented upstream for edb.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `edb --run /bin/ls` as documented upstream for edb.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `edb --attach 1720` as documented upstream for edb.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair edb with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `edb --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install edb-debugger. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `edb [OPTION]... [TARGET]`. Further invocations from upstream: `edb --symbols /lib/libc.so.6 > libc.so.6.map`; `edb --run /bin/ls`. edb - graphical debugger and disassembler for executables.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: edb - graphical debugger and disassembler for executables.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: edb - graphical debugger and disassembler for executables.
